title: Flash Fire During Piping Modification
description: A contractor crew was conducting welding on a 4-inch bypass pipeline under a Hot Work permit. A minor pocket of trapped gas escaped from the valve seat, triggering a local flash fire. The gas detector did not trigger early enough because it was positioned 5 meters away. Analysis showed localized CO and LEL levels had climbed over 15 minutes before the flare-up.
root_cause: Inadequate purging of the pipe spool combined with delayed local gas detection.
corrective_actions: Mandate local portable gas monitors within 1 meter of welding. Revise safety checks for hot work permits.

title: Toxic Gas Leak During Maintenance
description: An operator opened a flange on pump P-202 without verified Lockout Tagout (LOTO). Residual CO gas at 50 ppm escaped, resulting in minor inhalation symptoms. The sensor agent showed a sudden spike, but maintenance crew were not notified automatically since dashboard alerts were logged offline.
root_cause: Failure to apply LOTO and lack of real-time communication between safety dashboard and maintenance crews.
corrective_actions: Enforce LOTO validation prior to permit signoff. Implement automated safety officer push alerts.

title: O2 Deficiency Near Storage Tank T-102
description: A worker entering storage tank T-102 experienced lightheadedness. Handheld gas monitor recorded an O2 level of 18.2% (threshold: 19.5%). The entry was immediately halted and the worker was evacuated.
root_cause: Inadequate mechanical ventilation before and during the entry, despite confined space permits being active.
corrective_actions: Require continuous forced-air ventilation and secondary telemetry logs for storage tanks.

title: Arc Flash Near Substation 4 Transformer
description: During substation maintenance, an electrician touched a live transformer terminal. An arc flash occurred, causing minor hand burns. The LOTO checklist was signed off but not verified physically.
root_cause: Checking off checklists without field verification.
corrective_actions: Mandate live-line testing and physical key lock box systems for substation permits.

title: LEL Alarm During Cold Work Pipe Tapping
description: A cold tapping operation in Zone E was halted when a surrounding area sensor registered LEL at 12%. Work stopped, the area was cleared, and nitrogen purging was re-established.
root_cause: Gasket deterioration on neighboring line causing slow escape of butane gas.
corrective_actions: Conduct adjacent pipeline screening prior to starting work on shared platforms.
